Your message

I have a TypeScript project with strict mode enabled.

I'm getting an error: Object is possibly undefined.

How do I fix it without disabling strict mode?

Gemini's response

Use optional chaining (obj?.prop) or a null check (if (obj !== undefined)) to guard the access. You can also use the non-null assertion operator (obj!) if you are certain the value exists.
Your message

Which approach is best practice?

Gemini's response

Optional chaining is the cleanest approach. Use non-null assertion only when you have external guarantees the value exists that TypeScript cannot see.
